user_query: Plan 2 gym workouts for this week. I prefer morning sessions
user_profile: I'm Ido, 21 year old student. I have some spare time, I'd like to get into shape and lose 10-15 kg in the next year (My height  is 1.80 m and my weight is 150 kg). I enjoy cycling and boxing.

Monday.15:00: Afternoon: Low-Impact Cardio Intervals + Bodyweight Strength
Monday.19:00: Meal: Quick Chickpea & Spinach Coconut Curry

Tuesday.07:00: Tuesday Morning: Bodyweight Strength & Low-Impact Conditioning
Tuesday.13:00: Meal: Quick Black Bean & Corn Quesadilla

Wednesday.19:00: Meal: Little Italy Linguine With Chicken, Pesto and Pine Nuts

Thursday.07:00: Thursday Morning: Boxing-Style Conditioning & Core (No Equipment)
Thursday.19:00: Meal: Black Bean Soup With Chips and Salsa

Friday.07:00: Thursday Morning: Boxing Technique & Low-Impact Conditioning

nutrition.name: Quick Black Bean & Corn Quesadilla
nutrition.description: A speedy vegetarian Mexican main: black beans, corn and melty cheese tucked in whole-wheat tortillas, served with an easy avocado-lime crema. Ready in ~25 minutes.

ingredients.name: canned black beans, drained and rinsed

instructions: Heat a non-stick skillet over medium. In a bowl, lightly mash the rinsed black beans with a fork (leave some whole for texture). Stir in corn, cumin, chili powder, salt and pepper.
Make the avocado-lime crema: scoop avocado into a small bowl, add Greek yogurt, lime juice and half the cilantro; mash or blend until smooth. Set aside.
Lightly brush or wipe the skillet with olive oil. Place one tortilla in the skillet over medium heat.
Sprinkle about 1/4 cup shredded cheese over half the tortilla, top with 1/2 of the bean-corn mixture, then a little more cheese. Fold the tortilla over to make a half-moon.
Cook 2–3 minutes per side until golden brown and cheese is melted. Transfer to a cutting board and keep warm. Repeat with remaining tortillas and filling (makes 2 filled quesadillas, serves 2).
Slice each quesadilla into wedges. Serve topped with avocado-lime crema and remaining cilantro. Add lime wedges or hot sauce if desired.

nutrition.name: Chicken Mozzarella Panini With Red Peppers and Onions
nutrition.description: Quick panini with seasoned chicken, sautéed onions, roasted red peppers, mozzarella and pesto on ciabatta — ready in ~15 minutes.
instructions: Slice the chicken thinly, season with salt and pepper.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat; sauté sliced red onion until soft (2-3 minutes) and set aside.
Cook the chicken slices in the same skillet until no longer pink and cooked through, about 4-6 minutes.
Halve the ciabatta and spread pesto on the cut sides. Layer chicken, sautéed onions, roasted red peppers and mozzarella. Sprinkle oregano.
Grill the sandwich in a panini press or in a skillet with a weighted pan on top until bread is crisp and cheese melts, about 3-5 minutes.

nutrition.name: Little Italy Linguine With Chicken, Pesto and Pine Nuts
nutrition.description: A simple Italian-style linguine tossed with pesto, grilled chicken, lemon and toasted pine nuts — quick and satisfying for a Wednesday weeknight dinner.
instructions: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook linguine according to package directions until al dente.

While pasta cooks, season chicken with salt and pepper. Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat and cook chicken (sliced thin or cubed) 6–8 minutes until cooked through; remove and keep warm.

In the same skillet, add 1 tbsp butter and remaining olive oil; add a minced clove of garlic if desired and sauté ~30 seconds. Stir in pesto, lemon zest and juice, then add a splash of reserved pasta cooking water to loosen the sauce.

Drain pasta, reserving 1/2 cup cooking water. Toss linguine with the pesto sauce, grated parmesan, chopped basil and parsley, toasted pine nuts, and sliced chicken. Adjust seasoning and serve immediately.

nutrition.name: Quick Chicken & Vegetable Stir-Fry with Quick-Cook Brown Rice
nutrition.description: A fast, satisfying stir-fry of chicken and colorful vegetables served over quick-cook brown rice. Ready in ~30 minutes — perfect for a quick lunch main course.

ingredients.name: boneless skinless chicken breast, cut into 1-inch pieces

instructions: Start the quick-cook brown rice according to package directions (typically 10–15 minutes). Keep covered until serving.
While the rice cooks, toss the chicken pieces with cornstarch and a pinch of salt and pepper.

Heat olive oil in a large nonstick skillet or wok over medium-high heat. Add chicken in a single layer and cook, stirring, until browned and just cooked through, about 4–6 minutes. Remove chicken to a plate.
In the same pan, add sesame oil and sauté garlic for 20 seconds until fragrant. Add carrot slices and bell pepper; stir-fry 2 minutes.
Add broccoli florets and continue to stir-fry 2–3 minutes until vegetables are bright and tender-crisp.

Return the chicken to the pan. Add soy sauce and the juice of one lime; toss to coat and heat through (about 1–2 minutes). If you prefer a thicker sauce, add a splash of water and another 1/2 tsp cornstarch dissolved in 1 tbsp water, stirring until slightly thickened.
Finish by stirring in sliced green onions. Taste and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper if needed.
Serve the chicken and vegetables over the cooked quick-cook brown rice.

nutrition.name: Black Bean Soup With Chips and Salsa
nutrition.description: Hearty, quick Mexican-inspired black bean soup served with tortilla chips and fresh salsa-style garnishes — a filling vegetarian dinner ready in about 30 minutes.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add chopped onion, celery, and carrot; sauté until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add minced garlic and cook 30 seconds until fragrant.
Stir in chili powder and oregano, then add black beans, vegetable broth, and the bay leaf. Bring to a simmer and cook 10–12 minutes.
Remove and discard bay leaf. Blend about half the soup until smooth (use an immersion blender or carefully transfer to a blender), then return to the pot and stir to combine.
Stir in chopped tomato and lime juice; season to taste with salt.
Serve hot topped with diced avocado and sliced green onion, with tortilla chips on the side.

nutrition.name: Greek Yogurt Berry Parfait
nutrition.description: A light, dairy-forward breakfast parfait layered with plain Greek yogurt, mixed berries, crunchy granola, chia seeds, and a drizzle of honey. Ready in minutes and high in protein.
instructions: Spoon half the Greek yogurt into a serving glass or bowl.
Add a layer of mixed berries and sprinkle half the granola over them.
Top with the remaining yogurt, then add remaining berries and granola.
Sprinkle chia seeds and drizzle honey over the top. Serve immediately.

nutrition.name: Quick Chickpea & Spinach Coconut Curry
nutrition.description: A simple, vegetarian one-pan curry of chickpeas and spinach in a lightly spiced coconut-tomato sauce — ready in about 35 minutes and great with rice or naan.
instructions: Prep: peel and finely chop the onion; mince the garlic and ginger; drain and rinse the chickpeas.
Heat oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté until translucent, about 5–6 minutes.
Add the garlic and ginger and cook 1 minute until fragrant. Stir in cumin, turmeric, and red chili flakes and cook 30 seconds to bloom the spices.
Add the diced tomatoes and cook 3–4 minutes until the mixture thickens slightly.
Stir in the chickpeas and coconut milk. Bring to a gentle simmer and cook 6–8 minutes so flavors meld and sauce reduces slightly.
Fold in the spinach by handfuls, letting it wilt between additions. Cook 1–2 minutes more until all spinach is wilted and evenly distributed.
Season with salt and lime juice, taste and adjust seasoning. Sprinkle chopped cilantro over the curry and remove from heat.
Serve hot as-is or with steamed rice, quinoa, or flatbread.

nutrition.name: Chicken Enchilada Skillet
nutrition.description: Quick skillet enchilada-style chicken with torn corn tortillas, tomatoes, onion and melted cheese — a fast, satisfying Saturday dinner with meat and vegetables.
instructions: Heat a large skillet over medium heat and add olive oil.
Add chopped onion and sauté 3–4 minutes until softened.
Add the cooked shredded chicken and diced tomatoes; season with salt and pepper and cook 2–3 minutes until heated through.
Tear the corn tortillas into bite-sized pieces and add to the skillet, stirring to coat and warm them.
Sprinkle the shredded cheese evenly over the skillet, cover for 1–2 minutes until the cheese melts.
Stir gently to combine and serve hot. Optional: garnish with chopped cilantro, a squeeze of lime, or a dollop of plain yogurt or sour cream.

fitness.goal: Build foundational strength, increase daily calorie burn, and protect joints while starting steady weight loss.

items: Brisk march or easy jog in place — 2 minutes
Dynamic hip swings and leg swings — 1 minute each side
Arm circles + shoulder mobility — 2 minutes
Bodyweight glute bridges — 2 sets of 10 slow reps to wake hips
Ankle & knee mobility (controlled knee lifts) — 2 minutes
3 rounds circuit (perform each exercise then rest 60s between rounds):
A. Incline or knee push-ups — 8-12 reps
B. Box-free squats or sit-to-stand from a chair — 12-15 reps
C. Reverse lunges (alternating) or static split holds — 8-10 reps per leg
D. Glute bridges (single-leg progressions optional) — 12-15 reps
E. Plank (or knee-plank) — 30-45 seconds
F. Low-impact cardio finisher: marching high knees or step-ups — 60s
Slow walking or marching to bring HR down — 2 minutes
Seated hamstring and quad stretches — 3 minutes
Chest and shoulder stretch — 2 minutes
Deep diaphragmatic breathing + gentle spinal twists — 3 minutes

exercise_details.name: Incline or Knee Push-ups
exercise_details.notes: Use a wall or bench for incline if floor push-ups are too challenging. Keep core braced and avoid sagging hips.

exercise_details.name: Bodyweight Squats or Sit-to-Stand
exercise_details.notes: If knees feel sensitive, use a chair to perform sit-to-stand to reduce joint load. Focus on hip hinging and upright chest.

exercise_details.name: Reverse Lunges (alternating)
exercise_details.notes: Go shallow if balance or knees are an issue. Hold onto a stable surface for balance if needed.

exercise_details.name: Glute Bridges
exercise_details.notes: Squeeze glutes at the top; progress to single-leg bridges when comfortable.

exercise_details.name: Plank (or Knee-Plank)
exercise_details.notes: Keep a neutral spine; drop to knees for easier variation.

exercise_details.name: Low-Impact Cardio Finisher (marching high knees / step-ups)
exercise_details.notes: Keep intensity moderate; aim to raise heart rate without jarring the joints.

safety_notes: Begin at a comfortable intensity and progress week-to-week; prioritise controlled movement and breathing.
Given your current weight, favour low-impact options (incline push-ups, chair sit-to-stand, step-ups) to protect knees and lower back.
Stop and seek medical attention if you experience chest pain, dizziness, or unusual shortness of breath.
If you have any pre-existing conditions, check with a healthcare provider before beginning strenuous exercise.

I reduced strength load on Tuesday so Wednesday remains the main strength day, corrected the interval timing so the main section sums to 45 minutes, and added conservative progression and baseline safety checks before increasing intensity.

draft.goal: Activate joints and start the week with gentle movement to support recovery and consistency.

items: Neck rolls and shoulder circles (1 min)
Hip circles and ankle circles (1 min)
Marching on the spot and light arm swings (3 min)
Brisk walk 20 minutes at a conversational but purposeful pace (20 min)
Optional: 2 sets of 10 standing hip hinges for posterior chain activation (included in 20 min)
Standing hamstring and calf stretch
Calm deep breaths and shoulder relax

exercise_details.name: Brisk walk
exercise_details.notes: Keep pace comfortable but brisk; maintain upright posture and swing arms.

exercise_details.name: Standing hip hinge
exercise_details.notes: Hinge from hips, keep soft knee bend; use a chair for balance if needed.

safety_notes: Prioritize low-impact options to protect knees and hips given current body weight.
If any joint pain occurs, reduce duration and range of motion and consult a professional.

history.source_query: plan a workout for me on Tuesday, morning hours.
history.response_text: I created a full, beginner-friendly weekly plan with a focused Tuesday 07:00 morning boxing + low-impact interval session (60 min). I reduced strength load on Tuesday so Wednesday remains the main strength day, corrected the interval timing so the main section sums to 45 minutes, and added conservative progression and baseline safety checks before increasing intensity.

draft.goal: Capitalize on morning training benefits for weight management by combining boxing-style skill practice and low-impact interval cardio to raise energy expenditure while staying beginner-friendly and no-equipment. Reduce Tuesday strength load so recovery and a primary strength session remain later in the week.

items: Easy marching or light walk 3-4 minutes to raise heart rate
Dynamic mobility: shoulder circles, arm swings, hip openers, ankle rolls, gentle torso rotations (6-7 minutes)
Shadowboxing intervals: 6 rounds of 45s work / 45s easy active recovery = 18 minutes total (9 min work + 9 min recovery)

Light bodyweight skill circuit (reduced strength load): 2 rounds (each round ~6.5 minutes) total ~13 minutes: 8-10 bodyweight squats, 6-8 incline/wall push-ups, 8 glute bridges per round; rest ~60s between rounds
Controlled standing core & pacing: 2 rounds of 60s standing core rotations / light knee marches = 4 minutes (2 min work + 2 min easy)
Optional brisk 10-minute walk or extra recovery (to bring main to 45 minutes) = 10 minutes
Light walking and standing full-body stretches
2 minutes of diaphragmatic breathing to lower heart rate

exercise_details.name: Shadowboxing intervals (no equipment)
exercise_details.notes: Focus on jab-cross combos, light hooks, simple footwork and keeping hands up. Keep intensity at RPE 4-6/10. If balance or joint stress occurs, shorten rounds to 30-40s.

exercise_details.name: Reduced bodyweight circuit (Tuesday, light)
exercise_details.reps: per round: squats 8-10, incline push-ups 6-8, glute bridges 8
exercise_details.notes: Two rounds only on Tuesday to keep strength load low; prioritize form. This preserves Wednesday as the primary strength session.

exercise_details.name: Standing core rotations / light knee marches
exercise_details.notes: Control movement; coordinate breathing with rotation. Marching keeps heart rate managed during recovery.

exercise_details.name: Optional brisk walk (to reach 45 min main)
exercise_details.notes: Use as active recovery or to increase steady energy expenditure.

safety_notes: Research indicates that consistent morning exercise can support weight-management outcomes; scheduling this session at 07:00 may help adherence and fat-loss over time.
Given your current body mass, consider obtaining medical clearance before starting higher-intensity intervals or if you have symptoms (chest pain, dizziness, uncontrolled high blood pressure).

Baseline readiness check before starting interval progression: record resting heart rate and, if available, a recent blood pressure reading; consult primary care if resting BP is elevated or if you have cardiovascular risk factors.

Progression rules (conservative): only increase rounds or reduce rest if you meet all of the following: (a) RPE ≤ 6 for two consecutive similar sessions, (b) no joint pain or chest symptoms during or after sessions, and (c) you have completed at least 2-3 weeks at the current workload. Increase cadence to 2-3 weeks per increment. Maintain a minimum rest floor of 45 seconds for the first 8-12 weeks.
Require medical clearance before progressing to substantially higher intensity (for example: work/rest reductions below 45s, or adding many extra rounds).

Pre-session checklist: well-hydrated, light snack if needed, cleared space, supportive shoes, quick self-check for chest pain/dizziness; stop and seek care for chest pain, faintness, severe shortness of breath, or lightheadedness.

draft.goal: Build foundational strength, increase daily calorie burn, and protect joints while starting steady weight loss.

items: Brisk march or easy jog in place — 2 minutes
Dynamic hip swings and leg swings — 1 minute each side
Arm circles + shoulder mobility — 2 minutes
Bodyweight glute bridges — 2 sets of 10 slow reps to wake hips
Ankle & knee mobility (controlled knee lifts) — 2 minutes
3 rounds circuit (perform each exercise then rest 60s between rounds):
A. Incline or knee push-ups — 8-12 reps
B. Box-free squats or sit-to-stand from a chair — 12-15 reps
C. Reverse lunges (alternating) or static split holds — 8-10 reps per leg
D. Glute bridges (single-leg progressions optional) — 12-15 reps
E. Plank (or knee-plank) — 30-45 seconds
F. Low-impact cardio finisher: marching high knees or step-ups — 60s
Slow walking or marching to bring HR down — 2 minutes
Seated hamstring and quad stretches — 3 minutes
Chest and shoulder stretch — 2 minutes
Deep diaphragmatic breathing + gentle spinal twists — 3 minutes

exercise_details.name: Incline or Knee Push-ups
exercise_details.notes: Use a wall or bench for incline if floor push-ups are too challenging. Keep core braced and avoid sagging hips.

exercise_details.name: Bodyweight Squats or Sit-to-Stand
exercise_details.notes: If knees feel sensitive, use a chair to perform sit-to-stand to reduce joint load. Focus on hip hinging and upright chest.

exercise_details.name: Reverse Lunges (alternating)
exercise_details.notes: Go shallow if balance or knees are an issue. Hold onto a stable surface for balance if needed.

exercise_details.name: Glute Bridges
exercise_details.notes: Squeeze glutes at the top; progress to single-leg bridges when comfortable.

exercise_details.name: Plank (or Knee-Plank)
exercise_details.notes: Keep a neutral spine; drop to knees for easier variation.

exercise_details.name: Low-Impact Cardio Finisher (marching high knees / step-ups)
exercise_details.notes: Keep intensity moderate; aim to raise heart rate without jarring the joints.

safety_notes: Begin at a comfortable intensity and progress week-to-week; prioritise controlled movement and breathing.
Given your current weight, favour low-impact options (incline push-ups, chair sit-to-stand, step-ups) to protect knees and lower back.
Stop and seek medical attention if you experience chest pain, dizziness, or unusual shortness of breath.
If you have any pre-existing conditions, check with a healthcare provider before beginning strenuous exercise.

draft.goal: Improve aerobic fitness and coordination with boxing-style intervals and core work that suits a no-equipment morning session.

items: Light marching or easy hop/step in place — 2 minutes
Dynamic shoulder, neck and trunk rotations — 3 minutes
Shadowboxing slow rounds focusing on stance and jab-cross technique — 3 minutes
Ankle and hip loosening drills — 2 minutes
Interval blocks: 6 x (3-minute shadowboxing round at moderate intensity + 60s recovery). Focus on jab-cross, slip, and footwork.
Core-strength circuit (2 rounds): dead bugs 10-12, bird dogs 8-10 per side, side plank 20-30s per side.
Optional low-impact cardio bursts between core moves: mountain climbers (slow controlled) 30-40s or step-back burpees (no jump) 8-10 reps.

Standing quad and calf stretch — 3 minutes
Seated hamstring stretch and gentle spine twists — 3 minutes
Focused breathing to return HR to baseline — 2 minutes

exercise_details.name: Shadowboxing (moderate intensity rounds)
exercise_details.notes: Keep rounds controlled: focus on technique, rhythmic breathing, and light footwork. Move on the balls of the feet but avoid excessive bouncing if joints hurt.

exercise_details.name: Dead Bugs
exercise_details.notes: Keep lower back in contact with the floor; move slowly to build core stability.

exercise_details.name: Bird Dogs
exercise_details.notes: Perform with slow controlled movement; maintain a neutral spine.

exercise_details.name: Side Plank
exercise_details.notes: Drop to knee-side support if full side plank is too challenging.

exercise_details.name: Mountain Climbers (controlled) or Step-back Burpees (low-impact)
exercise_details.notes: Choose the lower-impact option (step-back burpee with no jump) if knees or back feel stressed.

safety_notes: Shadowboxing should be performed with good posture—don’t overreach on punches; use footwork to create distance instead.
Favor low-impact versions of cardio moves to reduce joint stress; avoid high-impact jumping if you feel discomfort.
Keep hydration and allow at least 24–48 hours between intense sessions if muscles feel overly sore.
If you have new or worsening joint pain, reduce range of motion or skip the offending exercise and consult a professional.

last_found_recipe.name: Chicken Enchilada Skillet
last_found_recipe.description: Quick skillet enchilada-style chicken with torn corn tortillas, tomatoes, onion and melted cheese — a fast, satisfying Saturday dinner with meat and vegetables.
instructions: Heat a large skillet over medium heat and add olive oil.
Add chopped onion and sauté 3–4 minutes until softened.
Add the cooked shredded chicken and diced tomatoes; season with salt and pepper and cook 2–3 minutes until heated through.
Tear the corn tortillas into bite-sized pieces and add to the skillet, stirring to coat and warm them.
Sprinkle the shredded cheese evenly over the skillet, cover for 1–2 minutes until the cheese melts.
Stir gently to combine and serve hot. Optional: garnish with chopped cilantro, a squeeze of lime, or a dollop of plain yogurt or sour cream.
